diagnostic.name: eager_xpu_decode_timing
diagnostic.purpose: Get region attribution for MiniMax M2.7 XPU decode. This is not an optimized throughput benchmark.
caveats: Uses eager execution, not the optimized AOT/XPU graph path.
Uses synchronized timing, so throughput is intentionally distorted.
Counts include the 512-token prefill plus 16 decode tokens.

findings: The eager synchronized diagnostic confirms MoE expert execution is the largest timed region, but max values include prefill and eager overhead.
Decode has many small allreduces. Rank 0 saw 2000 hidden-state all_reduce calls shaped (1,3072) averaging 0.170 ms each, plus 992 Q/K variance allreduces shaped (1,2) averaging 0.159 ms each.
The tiny Q/K variance allreduce has roughly the same per-call latency as the much larger decode hidden allreduce, so reducing collective count or fusing tiny collectives remains a high-value target.
AOT graph replay bypasses this Python timing helper, so optimized-path timing needs lower-level instrumentation or backend profiler traces.
next_targets: Investigate fusing MiniMax Q/K RMS variance allreduce with adjacent operations or combining Q and K variance communication into a larger existing collective.
Investigate allreduce plus residual/RMS epilogue fusion in XPU-supported form instead of Python custom-op dispatch.
Inspect llm-scaler INT4 MoE decode kernels and routing overhead for x.shape<=4; MoE remains the dominant compute region.
Instrument optimized graph path below Python, or add backend/communicator counters that survive graph replay.
